dedup.reason: Same tainted source (header:Stripe-Event-Id -> evtID) reaching two sinks (idempotency query :147 and audit insert :201). One root cause, surfaced by the single-pattern SAST rule and again by taint analysis. Merged so red-team validates one defect, not two — fixing source sanitization closes both sinks.
dedup.duplicate_cost_avoided: Validating both as separate PoCs would re-prove the same injection; ~50% of this finding's red-team budget saved by merging.

scoring_model: CVSS v4.0 base -> EPSS (FIRST) -> CISA KEV -> multi-factor priority matrix -> SLA (prioritizing-vulnerabilities-with-cvss-scoring Step 4-5; KEV/EPSS decision matrix)

findings.title: SQL injection via Stripe-Event-Id (idempotency query + audit insert)
findings.rationale: Network, no privileges, full read + write to the idempotency/audit tables. Critical by CVSS v4.0; reachable from the new endpoint.

findings.title: Forgeable webhook signature: leaked signing secret + non-constant-time HMAC compare
findings.rationale: Two findings kept distinct in code (rotate the secret AND fix the compare) but scored as one risk: either alone lets an attacker present a valid Stripe-Signature for attacker-authored events. This is the gate the whole endpoint's trust rests on.
findings.composed_from_note: Neither half is High alone in isolation-by-tool; the combination is. The arrow between scan_secrets and scan_crypto_timing is the severity.

findings.title: golang-jwt v3.2.0 algorithm confusion on the service-token check
findings.rationale: Reachable (webhook validates X-Internal-Token with the vulnerable parser). Not KEV, EPSS 0.18 -> P2 by the KEV+EPSS matrix; High and reachable, so a blocking candidate pending red-team reproduction.
findings.fix: Upgrade to jwt/v4 >= 4.4.3 + pin signing method.

findings.title: Out-of-bounds read in Stripe-Signature header parser
findings.rationale: Availability-only (panic -> 500). Real and remotely triggerable, but no confidentiality/integrity impact and not part of an exploit chain -> Medium, P4, ships as tracked debt.

findings.title: Insecure RNG for replay nonce
findings.rationale: math/rand nonce weakens replay protection but is not directly exploitable on its own. Low, P5.

handoff.note: Only the deduped blocking candidates go to the expensive red-team lane. T-004/T-005 are tracked, not emulated.
